steps.title: Identify the key deterrent
steps.content: The research shows poor lighting DETERS new or inexperienced cyclists but has little effect on experienced cyclists. This contrast shows which group would be affected by more streetlights.

steps.title: Apply the deterrent to the choices
steps.content: Removing poor lighting's deterrent effect means new or inexperienced cyclists should increase — choice D. Choices A, B, and C either contradict this outcome or focus on experienced cyclists, who weren't deterred by poor lighting. That matches choice D — D is correct.
